entries.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
entries.specific_competence: Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
entries.learning_activity: Explore the basic tenets of matrices ( 2×2 matrices: operations, determinant, inverse, and transformations)
teaching_learning_activities: Demonstration: Demonstrate the concept of matrices using real-life examples
Visual representation: Guide students to use graphs, charts, and tables to explain the concept of matrices
entries.assessment: The basic tenets of algebra are explored clearly
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
entries.specific_competence: Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
entries.learning_activity: Apply matrices to solve simultaneous equations of two unknowns (matrix inversion method and Cramer’ s rule)
teaching_learning_activities: Questions and answers: Organise students in manageable groups to determine the solution of simultaneous equations of two unknowns using matrix inversion method and Cramer’s rule

Use of technology: Guide students to use technology such as graphing calculators, and software such as Excel, Microsoft Mathematics, and MATLAB to determine the solutions of simultaneous equations with two unknowns using matrix inversion method and Cramer’s rule
Problem solving: In manageable groups, provide students with examples to practice basic operations on matrices, determinants, inverses, and transformations on matrices
entries.assessment: Matrices are clearly applied to solve simultaneous equations of two unknowns
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability, and statistics
entries.specific_competence: Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry, and vectors skills in daily life
entries.learning_activity: Explore the basic tenets of coordinate geometry (midpoint of a line segment, distance between two points on a line, parallel, and perpendicular lines)
teaching_learning_activities: Demonstration: Demonstrate the concepts of midpoint of a line segment, distance between two points on a line, parallel lines, and perpendicular lines using real-life problems
Problem solving: Organise students in manageable groups to use real world examples to find the midpoint of a line segment, distance between two points on a line, parallel, and perpendicular lines

Visual representation: Guide students to use graphs, charts, and real objects to explain the concepts of midpoint of a line segment, distance between two points on a line, parallel lines, and perpendicular lines
entries.assessment: The basic tenets of coordinate geometry are well explored
resources: Mathematical software such as GeoGebra, Mathematica, MATLAB, Maple
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability, and statistics
entries.specific_competence: Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry, and vectors skills in daily life
entries.learning_activity: Apply sine and cosine rules to find distances or angles of elevation
teaching_learning_activities: Exploration: Guide students to explore the application of sine and cosine rules in finding distances or angles of elevation
Use of technology: Guide students to use technology such as graphing calculators, and software such as Excel and MATLAB to apply sine and cosine rules to find distances or angles of elevation
Problem solving: In manageable groups, provide students with questions to practice on how to apply the sine and cosine rules to determine the distance or angles of elevation
entries.assessment: The sine and cosine rules are clearly applied to find distances and angles of elevation
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.specific_competence: Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry, and vectors skills in daily life
entries.learning_activity: Derive and use compound angles to solve problems
teaching_learning_activities: Group discussion: Organise students in manageable groups to derive and use compound angles to solve problems
Use of technology: Guide students to use technology such as graphing calculators and compound angles to solve problems
Jigsaw: In manageable groups, provide students with assignment to solve problems
entries.assessment: The compound angles are clearly derived and used to solve problems

entries.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability, and statistics
entries.specific_competence: Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry, and vectors skills in daily life
entries.learning_activity: Explore the basic tenets of vectors (displacement and position vectors, magnitude and direction, sum and differences, multiplication of vectors by a scalar)
teaching_learning_activities: Exploration: Encourage students individually or in groups to explore the basic tenets of vectors through visual representations, problem solving and its applications in real life
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability, and statistics
entries.specific_competence: Use probability in problem solving
entries.learning_activity: Explore the basic tenets of probability of two events (probability of an event, mutually exclusive events, dependent events, combined events using tree diagrams, tables and formulae)
teaching_learning_activities: Visual representation: Use interactive tools to demonstrate concept of probability of an event
Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss and determine probability of an event, dependent event, combined events using tree diagram, tables and formulae
entries.assessment: The basic tenets of probability are well explored
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability, and statistics
entries.specific_competence: Use statistics in problem solving
entries.learning_activity: Explore the basic tenets of statistics (frequency distribution, measures of central tendency, histogram, frequency polygon, and cumulative frequency curve ‘ogive’)
teaching_learning_activities: Demonstration: Using real-life examples, guide the students to describe and demonstrate the concepts of frequency distribution and measure of central tendency
Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss and determine the measures of central tendency, draw histogram, frequency polygon and cumulative frequency curve (ogive)
Exploration: Guide students in groups to explore the basic tenets of statistics through the use of technology, visual aids such as charts of data and graphs of real life scenarios
entries.assessment: The basic tenets of statistics are explored clearly
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability, and statistics

teaching_learning_activities: Demonstration: Using real-life examples, guide the students to describe and demonstrate the concepts of frequency distribution and measure of central tendency
Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss and determine the measures of central tendency, draw histogram, frequency polygon and cumulative frequency curve (ogive)
Exploration: Guide students in groups to explore the basic tenets of statistics through the use of technology, visual aids such as charts of data and graphs of real life scenarios
entries.assessment: The basic tenets of statistics are explored clearly
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education
